title: batrachium aquatile subsp. caespitosum
intro: Batrachium aquatile subsp. caespitosum, also known as the water crowfoot or marsh marigold, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Batrachium aquatile subsp. caespitosum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as a diuretic, making it beneficial for treating urinary tract issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be dried and used in salads or as a garnish.
